entities.name: ROOT ANGLE
entities.description: The angle at which roots grow, influencing the efficiency of nutrient and water capture from soil layers

entities.name: NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Essential chemical elements required for plant growth and development

entities.name: WATER
entities.description: Essential liquid resource for plant survival and growth

entities.name: SOIL LAYERS
entities.description: The various depths of soil where water and nutrients are distributed

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A mobile nutrient typically accessed from deeper soil layers via steeper root angles

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: An immobile nutrient typically captured from the topsoil via shallow root angles

entities.name: TOPSOIL
entities.description: The uppermost layer of soil where immobile phosphorus is concentrated

entities.name: GENETIC REGULATION
entities.description: The biological process by which genes control the expression of traits like root angle

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The total agricultural productivity of a crop variety

entities.name: CARBON
entities.description: An element that can be sequestered in deeper soil layers to aid environmental efforts

entities.name: CARBON MITIGATION
entities.description: Global efforts to reduce atmospheric carbon levels to combat climate change

entities.name: MOLECULAR COMPONENTS
entities.description: The specific genes or proteins that control the development of root angle

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: A group of grass-like plants grown for their edible seeds, the subject of the root angle study
